Article.ArticleTitle: The Generalized Harmonic Mean for p $$ p $$ -Values: Combining Dependent and Independent Tests.

Abstract.AbstractText: In medical research, particularly in fields such as genomics, multi-center clinical trials, and meta-analysis, effectively combining the p-values from multiple related hypothesis tests has always been a challenging statistical issue. To address this problem and enhance the statistical power of comprehensive analysis, this study proposes a generalized harmonic mean for p-values (GHMP( ξ $$ \xi $$ )) combination method and builds two kinds of combination tests based on this framework. The first kind of test is designed for applications with small significance levels and has more lenient conditions for adapting to correlations, making it suitable for the complex dependency structures commonly found in actual research. The second kind of test introduces a novel high-order tail approximation technique based on stable distribution theory, which can more accurately estimate the extreme tail probabilities at large significance levels under independent or weakly correlated conditions. Extensive simulation experiments show that both kinds of tests perform robustly across various configurations, with statistical power not inferior to the traditional Cauchy combination test (CCT) and minimum p-value (MinP) methods, and demonstrate superior detection capabilities in several scenarios. Additionally, GHMP( ξ $$ \xi $$ ) has high computational efficiency and has been empirically validated in real genetic data. These characteristics make it a reliable and practical analytical tool for high-dimensional medical research, such as genome-wide association studies (GWAS) and large-scale meta-analysis.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: School of Mathematics and Statistics, Key Laboratory of NAA-MOE, Central China Normal University, Wuhan, China.
School of Mathematics and Statistics, Key Laboratory of NAA-MOE, Central China Normal University, Wuhan, China.

Grant.Agency: Key Laboratory of Nonlinear Analysis and Applications (Central China Normal University) and Ministry of Education, People's Republic of China

Reference.Citation: A. Okbay, B. M. Baselmans, J. E. De Neve, et al., “Genetic Variants Associated With Subjective Well‐Being, Depressive Symptoms, and Neuroticism Identified Through Genome‐Wide Analyses,” Nature Genetics 48, no. 6 (2016): 624–633.
R. A. Fisher, “Statistical Methods for Research Workers,” 1934.

K. Pearson, “On a Method of Determining Whether a Sample of Size n Supposed to Have Been Drawn From a Parent Population Having a Known Probability Integral Has Probably Been Drawn at Random,” Biometrika 25 (1933): 379–410.
G. S. Mudholkar and E. George, “The Logit Statistic for Combining Probabilities–An Overview,” 1977.
E. S. Edgington, “An Additive Method for Combining Probability Values From Independent Experiments,” Journal of Psychology 80, no. 2 (1972): 351–363.

S. A. Stouffer, E. A. Suchman, L. C. DeVinney, S. A. Star, and R. M. Williams, Jr., The American Soldier: Adjustment During Army Life (Studies in Social Psychology in World War II), vol. 1 (Princeton University Press, 1949).
L. H. C. Tippett, “The Methods of Statistics,” 1941.

Y. Liu and J. Xie, “Cauchy Combination Test: A Powerful Test With Analytic p‐Value Calculation Under Arbitrary Dependency Structures,” Journal of the American Statistical Association 115, no. 529 (2020): 393–402.
M. Long, Z. Li, W. Zhang, and Q. Li, “The Cauchy Combination Test Under Arbitrary Dependence Structures,” American Statistical 77, no. 2 (2023): 134–142.
Y. Fang, C. Chang, Y. Park, and G. C. Tseng, “Heavy‐Tailed Distribution for Combining Dependent p‐Values With Asymptotic Robustness,” Statistica Sinica 33 (2023): 1115–1142.
D. J. Wilson, “The Harmonic Mean p‐Value for Combining Dependent Tests,” National Academy of Sciences of The United States of America 116, no. 4 (2019): 1195–1200.
T. Liu, X. L. Meng, and N. S. Pillai, “A Heavily Right Strategy for Integrating Dependent Studies in Any Dimension,” arXiv preprint, arXiv:2501.01065 (2025).
A. Birnbaum, “Combining Independent Tests of Significance,” Journal of The American Statistical Association 49, no. 267 (1954): 559–574.
L. Gui, Y. Jiang, and J. Wang, “Aggregating Dependent Signals With Heavy‐Tailed Combination Tests,” Biometrika 112 (2025): asaf038.
V. M. Zolotarev, One‐Dimensional Stable Distributions, vol. 65 (American Mathematical Socity, 1986).
B. V. Gnedenko and A. N. Kolmogorov, Limit Distributions for Sums of Independent Random Variables, Addison‐Wesley Series in Statistics Reading (Addison‐Wesley Pub. Co., 1954).
V. V. Uchaikin and V. M. Zolotarev, Chance and Stability: Stable Distributions and Their Applications (Walter de Gruyter, 2011).
K. S. Kölbig and B. Schorr, “A Program Package for the Landau Distribution,” Computer Physics Communications 31, no. CERN‐DD‐83‐18 (1983): 97–111.
M. B. Brown, “400: A Method for Combining Non‐Independent, One‐Sided Tests of Significance,” Biometrics 31 (1975): 987–992.
R. Weron, “On the Chambers‐Mallows‐Stuck Method for Simulating Skewed Stable Random Variables,” Statistics & Probability Letters 28, no. 2 (1996): 165–171.
J. P. Nolan, “Numerical Calculation of Stable Densities and Distribution Functions,” Communications in Statistics. Stochastic Models 13, no. 4 (1997): 759–774.
M. Teimouri and H. Amindavar, “A Novel Approach to Calculate Stable Densities,” in Proceedings of the World Congress on Engineering, vol. 1 (WCE, 2008).
S. Ament and M. O'Neil, “Accurate and Efficient Numerical Calculation of Stable Densities via Optimized Quadrature and Asymptotics,” Statistics and Computing 28, no. 1 (2018): 171–185.
D. Donoho and J. Jin, “Higher Criticism for Detecting Sparse Heterogeneous Mixtures,” 2004.

S. De Vito, E. Massera, M. Piga, L. Martinotto, and G. Di Francia, “On Field Calibration of an Electronic Nose for Benzene Estimation in an Urban Pollution Monitoring Scenario,” Sensors and Actuators B: Chemical 129, no. 2 (2008): 750–757.
J. Runge, International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (PMLR, 2018), 938–947.
P. Wainschtein, D. Jain, Z. Zheng, et al., “Assessing the Contribution of Rare Variants to Complex Trait Heritability From Whole‐Genome Sequence Data,” Nature Genetics 54, no. 3 (2022): 263–273.

M. Shintani and K. Umeno, “Super Generalized Central Limit Theorem–Limit Distributions for Sums of Non‐Identical Random Variables With Power Laws,” Journal of the Physical Society of Japan 87, no. 4 (2018): 043003.
